name: Pumpkin Walnut Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend the crushed Zwieback crackers, 1/4 cup sugar, and 6 tablespoons melted butter.
2. Press the mixture firmly over the bottom and up the sides of a lightly buttered 9-inch spring-form pan. Chill.
3. Beat the cream cheese until smooth. Add the 3/4 cup sugar and the 3/4 cup brown sugar, beating until well mixed.
4. Beat in the eggs one at a time until the mixture is light and fluffy. Beat in the pumpkin pie spice and heavy cream at low speed.
5. Mix in the pumpkin.
6. Pour into the prepared pan.
7. Bake in a slow oven (325°F) for one hour and 35 minutes.
8. While the pie is baking, mix the topping ingredients: first combine the butter and brown sugar until crumbly, then blend in the walnuts.
9. After one hour and 35 minutes, remove the pie from the oven. Spread the topping over it and return to the oven for 10 minutes.
10. Remove from the oven and cool on a wire rack. Refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
This cheesecake is rather large and incredibly rich. Everyone always wants more than they can fit in their stomach! And the recipe! Serving suggestion: Some like this garnished with whipped cream and more walnuts, or with whipped cream and pecans.
